text: Maximal oxygen consumption does not differ significantly between treadmill running, elliptical trainer, and stair stepper exercise in trained runners.
population: trained runners (male and female, ~23 years old)

text: Heart rate response at matched submaximal intensities (60%, 70%, 80% of peak) does not differ between treadmill running, elliptical trainer, and stair stepper, making heart rate a valid intensity guide across these modalities.
population: trained runners (male and female, ~23 years old)

text: Carbohydrate and fat oxidation rates at matched submaximal intensities do not differ between treadmill running, elliptical trainer, and stair stepper, indicating equivalent fuel utilization across modalities.
population: trained runners (male and female, ~23 years old)

text: Elliptical trainers and stair steppers are suitable aerobic substitutes for running during injury rehabilitation periods requiring reduced running load, as they produce equivalent physiological responses at matched intensities.
Elliptical trainers and stair steppers impose reduced stress on muscles and joints compared to running while maintaining equivalent oxygen consumption and energy expenditure.
